title: Loved at Your Worst
reading:
The most disarming thing about God's validation is its timing. Romans 5:8 — while we were still sinners, Christ died for us. Not after we cleaned up. Not after we proved ourselves. While we were still in the mess.

This is what separates God's approval from human approval. People typically approve of you when you perform well, when you're useful, when you're easy to be around. God approved of you when you were none of those things. His love moved toward you when you were, by your own record, undeserving.

Revelation 1:5-6 builds on this: He loved us, washed us from our sins in His own blood, and made us kings and priests. Past tense. Finished. The washing is complete. The making is done. You don't carry the status of a sinner hoping to be accepted — you carry the status of a priest standing before God.

1 Peter 5:6-7 captures the posture this produces: humble yourself under God's mighty hand, casting all your care on Him — because He cares for you. He cares. Not because you performed. Because you are His.

This is the foundation that makes people pleasing unnecessary. When you know you are loved like this, you no longer need to earn it from others.
reflection: Do you carry your burdens as if God is indifferent, or do you cast them as someone who knows He cares? What would it look like to fully receive His love today?
abide: Jesus, thank You for loving me at my worst. Help me receive that love fully — so I stop looking for it in places where it cannot be found.
